##NAMES=Volkovskite
##RRUFFID=R110151
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=KCa_4_B_22_O_32_(OH)_10_Cl·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Potash Corporation of Saskatchewan mine, Penobsquis, Kings County, New Brunswick, Canada
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Donald M Doell and Brian Roulston
##DESCRIPTION=Pale red platy crystals, extracted by dissolving the matrix of sylvite/halite, associated with strontioginorite R110149, howlite R110150, ruitenbergite R110152, congolite R110153, and hilgardite
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R110151
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 6.538 b: 23.96 c: 6.5862 alpha: 95.49 beta: 119.176 gamma: 90.62 volume: 894.8 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
